Thread an optional `doc` (default `document`) through embedCustomFonts() and ensureFontsReady(), and pass state.element.ownerDocument from capture.js. This mirrors how measurement was made iframe-aware in #371. Adds a same-origin iframe test covering both the inline

web.markdown:
# @mindblowing/snapdom  ![TypeScript icon, indicating that this package has built-in type declarations](https://static-production.npmjs.com/4a2a680dfcadf231172b78b1d3beb975.svg)

1.9.9 • Public • Published a year ago

- [Readme](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom?activeTab=readme)
- [Code Beta](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom?activeTab=code)
- [0 Dependencies](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom?activeTab=dependencies)
- [0 Dependents](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom?activeTab=dependents)
- [2 Versions](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom?activeTab=versions)

# snapDOM

[Permalink: snapDOM](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#snapdom)

[![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zumerlab/snapdom/main/docs/assets/hero.png)](http://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom)

[![NPM version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/86bb80230e3f98205c29a9727c4338ba828ce1ee5298e767e197bd6e52d85f74/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f762f407a756d65722f736e6170646f6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d56657273696f6e)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zumer/snapdom)[![GitHub contributors](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/2292687faa0fec681ab8fa8bac76268c50bfe9c04b74cf663804443fe45d6a57/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f636f6e7472696275746f72732f7a756d65726c61622f736e6170646f6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d436f6e7472696275746f7273)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/graphs/contributors)[![GitHub stars](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/080a5fe91576c3dcdb42d76c0d893202f1bf8aab34fe8ebcfc92b044178de5cd/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f7a756d65726c61622f736e6170646f6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d5374617273)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/stargazers)[![GitHub forks](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b35f7aa11429701f72195b3b5978c1aa72cea40f8fb465fee0fffc2b7a1838d6/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f666f726b732f7a756d65726c61622f736e6170646f6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d466f726b73)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/network/members)[![Sponsor tinchox5](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/26a4bcf5d2113e1d646eda97b7802dd22dbfa8843e36392059c1dfe933dc8668/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73706f6e736f72732f74696e63686f78353f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265266c6162656c3d53706f6e736f72)](https://github.com/sponsors/tinchox5)[![License](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9ff17440e08665142c5be71a94743355ec9f1fdd5ddb4e08e31d7f07758fdfab/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f6c6963656e73652f7a756d65726c61622f736e6170646f6d3f7374796c653d666c61742d737175617265)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/blob/main/LICENSE)

**snapDOM** is a fast and accurate DOM-to-image capture tool built for **Zumly**, a zoom-based view transition framework.

It captures any HTML element as a scalable SVG image, preserving styles, fonts, background images, pseudo-elements, and even shadow DOM. It also supports export to raster image formats and canvas.

- 📸 Full DOM capture
- 🎨 Embedded styles, pseudo-elements, and fonts
- 🖼️ Export to SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, or `canvas`
- ⚡ Ultra fast, no dependencies
- 📦 100% based on standard Web APIs

## Demo

[Permalink: Demo](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#demo)

[https://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom/](https://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom/)

## Installation

[Permalink: Installation](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#installation)

### NPM / Yarn

[Permalink: NPM / Yarn](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#npm--yarn)

```
npm i @zumer/snapdom
```

```
yarn add @zumer/snapdom
```

### CDN

[Permalink: CDN](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#cdn)

```
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@zumer/snapdom/dist/snapdom.min.js"></script>
```

### Script tag (local)

[Permalink: Script tag (local)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#script-tag-local)

```
<script src="snapdom.js"></script>
```

### ES Module

[Permalink: ES Module](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#es-module)

```
import { snapdom } from './snapdom.mjs';
```

### Module via CDN

[Permalink: Module via CDN](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#module-via-cdn)

```
<script type="module">
  import { snapdom } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@zumer/snapdom/dist/snapdom.mjs';
</script>
```

## Basic usage

[Permalink: Basic usage](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#basic-usage)

### Reusable capture

[Permalink: Reusable capture](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#reusable-capture)

```
const el = document.querySelector('#target');
const result = await snapdom(el, { scale: 2 });

const img = await result.toPng();
document.body.appendChild(img);

await result.download({ format: 'jpg', filename: 'my-capture' });
```

### One-step shortcuts

[Permalink: One-step shortcuts](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#one-step-shortcuts)

```
const el = document.querySelector('#target');
const png = await snapdom.toPng(el);
document.body.appendChild(png);

const blob = await snapdom.toBlob(el);
```

## API

[Permalink: API](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#api)

### `snapdom(el, options?)`

[Permalink: snapdom(el, options?)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#snapdomel-options)

Returns an object with reusable export methods:

```
{
  url: string;
  toRaw(): string;
  toImg(): Promise<HTMLImageElement>;
  toCanvas(): Promise<HTMLCanvasElement>;
  toBlob(): Promise<Blob>;
  toPng(): Promise<HTMLImageElement>;
  toJpg(options?): Promise<HTMLImageElement>;
  toWebp(options?): Promise<HTMLImageElement>;
  download(options?): Promise<void>;
}
```

### Shortcut methods

[Permalink: Shortcut methods](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#shortcut-methods)

| Method | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `snapdom.toImg(el, options?)` | Returns an `HTMLImageElement` |
| `snapdom.toCanvas(el, options?)` | Returns a `Canvas` |
| `snapdom.toBlob(el, options?)` | Returns an SVG `Blob` |
| `snapdom.toPng(el, options?)` | Returns a PNG image |
| `snapdom.toJpg(el, options?)` | Returns a JPG image |
| `snapdom.toWebp(el, options?)` | Returns a WebP image |
| `snapdom.download(el, options?)` | Triggers download in specified format |

## Options

[Permalink: Options](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#options)

All capture methods accept an `options` object:

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `compress` | boolean | `true` | Removes redundant styles |
| `fast` | boolean | `true` | Skips idle delay for faster results |
| `embedFonts` | boolean | `false` | Inlines fonts (icon fonts always embedded) |
| `scale` | number | `1` | Output scale multiplier |
| `width` | number | - | Output specific width size |
| `height` | number | - | Output specific height size |
| `backgroundColor` | string | `"#fff"` | Fallback color for JPG/WebP |
| `quality` | number | `1` | Quality for JPG/WebP (0 to 1) |
| `useProxy` | string | '' | Specify a proxy for handling CORS images as fallback |
| `type` | string | `svg` | Select `png`, `jpg`, `webp` Blob type |
| `exclude` | string\[\] | - | CSS selectors for elements to exclude |
| `filter` | function | - | Custom filter function ie `(el) => !el.classList.contains('hidden')` |

### Setting custom dimensions with width and height options

[Permalink: Setting custom dimensions with width and height options](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#setting-custom-dimensions-with-width-and-height-options)

Use the `width` and `height` options to generate an image with specific dimensions.

**Examples:**

**1\. Fixed width (proportional height)**
Sets a specific width while maintaining the aspect ratio. Height adjusts proportionally.

```
const result = await snapdom(element, {
  width: 400 // Outputs a 400px-wide image with auto-scaled height
});
```

**2\. Fixed height (proportional width)**
Sets a specific height while maintaining the aspect ratio. Width adjusts proportionally.

```
const result = await snapdom(element, {
  height: 200 // Outputs a 200px-tall image with auto-scaled width
});
```

**3\. Fixed width and height (may distort image)**
Forces exact dimensions, potentially distorting the image if the aspect ratio differs.

```
const result = await snapdom(element, {
  width: 800,  // Outputs an 800px × 200px image (may stretch/squish content)
  height: 200
});
```

**Note:** If `scale` is different from 1, it takes priority over width and height.
Example: `{ scale: 3, width: 500 }` ignores width and scales the image 3x instead.

### Cross-Origin Images

[Permalink: Cross-Origin Images](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#cross-origin-images)

By default, snapDOM loads images with `crossOrigin="anonymous"` or `crossOrigin="use-credentials"`. In case fails to get the images, `useProxy` can be used to deal with CORS images:

```
const result = await snapdom(element, {
  useProxy: 'your/proxy/' //Example: 'https://corsproxy.io/?url=' or 'https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url='
});
```

### Download options

[Permalink: Download options](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#download-options)

```
{
  format?: "svg" | "png" | "jpg" | "jpeg" | "webp"; // default: "png"
  filename?: string;         // default: "capture"
  backgroundColor?: string;  // optional override
}
```

### `preCache()` – Optional helper

[Permalink: preCache() – Optional helper](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#precache--optional-helper)

The `preCache()` function can be used to load external resources (like images and fonts) in advance. It is specially useful when the element to capure is big and complex.

```
import { preCache } from '@zumer/snapdom';

await preCache(document.body);
```

```
import { snapdom, preCache } from './snapdom.mjs';
    window.addEventListener('load', async () => {
    await preCache();
    console.log('📦 Resources preloaded');
    });
```

**Options for `preCache()`:**

- `embedFonts` _(boolean, default: true)_ — Inlines non-icon fonts during preload.
- `reset` _(boolean, default: false)_ — Clears all existing internal caches.
- `useProxy` _(string)_ — Proxy for handling CORS images as fallback.

## Features

[Permalink: Features](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#features)

- Captures **shadow DOM** and Web Components
- Supports `::before`, `::after` and `::first-letter` pseudo-elements
- Inlines background images and fonts
- Handles **Font Awesome**, **Material Icons**, and more
- `data-capture="exclude"` to ignore an element
- `data-capture="placeholder"` with `data-placeholder-text` for masked replacements

## Limitations

[Permalink: Limitations](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#limitations)

- External images shloud be CORS-accessible (use `useProxy` option for handling CORS denied)
- Iframes are not supported
- When WebP format is used on Safari, it will fallback to PNG rendering.
- `@font-face` CSS rule is well supported, but if need to use JS `FontFace()`, see this workaround [`#43`](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/issues/43)

## ⚡ Performance Benchmarks

[Permalink: ⚡ Performance Benchmarks](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#-performance-benchmarks)

Snapdom has received **significant performance improvements** since version `v1.8.0`. The following benchmarks compare:

- **Snapdom (current)**
- **Snapdom v1.8.0**
- `html2canvas`
- `html-to-image`

### Simple elements

[Permalink: Simple elements](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#simple-elements)

| Scenario | Snapdom (current) | Snapdom v1.8.0 | html2canvas | html-to-image |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Small (200×100) | **0.4 ms** | 1.2 ms | 70.3 ms | 3.6 ms |
| Modal (400×300) | **0.4 ms** | 1.1 ms | 68.8 ms | 3.6 ms |
| Page View (1200×800) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 100.5 ms | 3.4 ms |
| Large Scroll (2000×1500) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 153.1 ms | 3.4 ms |
| Very Large (4000×2000) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 278.9 ms | 4.3 ms |

### Complex elements

[Permalink: Complex elements](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#complex-elements)

| Scenario | Snapdom (current) | Snapdom v1.8.0 | html2canvas | html-to-image |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Small (200×100) | **1.1 ms** | 3.2 ms | 76.0 ms | 15.3 ms |
| Modal (400×300) | **4.5 ms** | 14.0 ms | 133.2 ms | 55.4 ms |
| Page View (1200×800) | **32.9 ms** | 113.6 ms | 303.4 ms | 369.1 ms |
| Large Scroll (2000×1500) | **133.9 ms** | 387.4 ms | 594.4 ms | 1,163.0 ms |
| Very Large (4000×2000) | **364.0 ms** | 1,200.4 ms | 1,380.8 ms | 3,023.9 ms |

### Summary

[Permalink: Summary](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#summary)

- Snapdom (current) is **2×–6× faster** than `v1.8.0`
- Up to **150× faster** than `html2canvas`
- Up to **8× faster** than `html-to-image` in large scenarios

Benchmarks run in Chromium using Vitest.

Hardware: MacBook Air 2018.

⚠️ Performance may vary depending on device.

### Run the benchmarks

[Permalink: Run the benchmarks](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#run-the-benchmarks)

```
git clone https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom.git
cd snapdom
npm install
npm run test:benchmark
```

## Development

[Permalink: Development](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#development)

To contribute or build snapDOM locally:

```
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom.git
cd snapdom

# Switch to dev branch
git checkout dev

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Compile the library (ESM, CJS, and minified versions)
npm run compile

# Install playwright browsers (necessary for running tests)
npx playwright install

# Run tests
npm test

# Run Benchmarks
npm run test:benchmark
```

The main entry point is in `src/`, and output bundles are generated in the `dist/` folder.

For detailed contribution guidelines, please see [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

## Contributors 🙌

[Permalink: Contributors 🙌](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#contributors-)

[![tinchox5](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11557901?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/tinchox5 "tinchox5")[![tarwin](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/646149?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/tarwin "tarwin")[![17biubiu](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13295895?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/17biubiu "17biubiu")[![pedrocateexte](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/207524750?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/pedrocateexte "pedrocateexte")[![domialex](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4694217?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/domialex "domialex")[![elliots](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/622455?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/elliots "elliots")[![jswhisperer](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1177690?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/jswhisperer "jswhisperer")[![simon1uo](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60037549?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/simon1uo "simon1uo")[![titoBouzout](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64156?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/titoBouzout "titoBouzout")[![jhbae200](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20170610?v=4&s=100)](https://github.com/jhbae200 "jhbae200")

## 💖 Sponsors

[Permalink: 💖 Sponsors](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mindblowing/snapdom#-sponsors)

Special thanks to [@megaphonecolin](https://github.com/megaphonecolin) for supporting this project!

If you'd like to support this project too, you can [become a sponsor](https://github.com/sponsors/tinchox5).

metadata.ogDescription: snapDOM captures HTML elements to images with exceptional speed and accuracy.. Latest version: 1.9.9, last published: a year ago. Start using @mindblowing/snapdom in your project by running `npm i @mindblowing/snapdom`. There are no other projects in the npm registry using @mindblowing/snapdom.

web.markdown: [Skip to main content](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2#main-content)@zumer/snapdom - npmx 1.9.10-dev.2 Fast, modern alternative to html2canvas — captures HTML elements to images (PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP) with exceptional speed and accuracy. - [zumerlab/snapdom](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom) - [7.9K](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/stargazers) - [288](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/forks) - [homepage](https://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom/) - [issues](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/issues) - [socket.dev](https://socket.dev/npm/package/@zumer/snapdom "View on socket.dev") - [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zumer/snapdom "View on npm") - Types - ESM - CJS License[MIT](https://spdx.org/licenses/MIT.html "View license text on SPDX") Deps0 Install Size 367.1 kB Vulns 0 PublishedSep 8, 2025 ## [Get started](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#get-started) Download npmpnpmyarnbundenovltvp $`npm install @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`pnpm add @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`yarn add @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`bun add @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`deno add npm:@zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`vlt install @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy $`vp add @zumer/snapdom@1.9.10-dev.2`copy ## [Weekly Downloads](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#downloads) Across all versions Trends Use the left and right arrow keys to cycle through data points. [![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zumerlab/snapdom/main/docs/assets/newhero.png)](http://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom) [![NPM version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@zumer/snapdom?style=flat-square&label=Version)](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom)[![GitHub contributors](https://img.shields.io/github/contributors/zumerlab/snapdom?style=flat-square&label=Contributors)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/graphs/contributors)[![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/zumerlab/snapdom?style=flat-square&label=Stars)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/stargazers)[![GitHub forks](https://img.shields.io/github/forks/zumerlab/snapdom?style=flat-square&label=Forks)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/network/members)[![Sponsor tinchox5](https://img.shields.io/github/sponsors/tinchox5?style=flat-square&label=Sponsor)](https://github.com/sponsors/tinchox5)[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/zumerlab/snapdom?style=flat-square)](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/blob/main/LICENSE) ### [snapDOM](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-snapdom) **snapDOM** is a fast and accurate DOM-to-image capture tool built for **Zumly**, a zoom-based view transition framework. It captures any HTML element as a scalable SVG image, preserving styles, fonts, background images, pseudo-elements, and even shadow DOM. It also supports export to raster image formats and canvas. - 📸 Full DOM capture - 🎨 Embedded styles, pseudo-elements, and fonts - 🖼️ Export to SVG, PNG, JPG, WebP, `canvas`, or Blob - ⚡ Ultra fast, no dependencies - 📦 100% based on standard Web APIs #### [Demo](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-demo) [https://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom/](https://zumerlab.github.io/snapdom/) #### [Installation](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-installation) ##### [NPM / Yarn](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-npm-yarn) Copy code ``` npm i @zumer/snapdom ``` Copy code ``` yarn add @zumer/snapdom ``` Then import it in your code: Copy code ``` import { snapdom } from '@zumer/snapdom'; ``` ##### [CDN](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-cdn) Copy code ``` ``` ##### [Script tag (local)](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-script-tag-local) Copy code ``` ``` ##### [ES Module](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-es-module) Copy code ``` import { snapdom } from './snapdom.mjs'; ``` ##### [Module via CDN](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-module-via-cdn) Copy code ``` ``` #### [Basic usage](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-basic-usage) ##### [Reusable capture](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-reusable-capture) Copy code ``` const el = document.querySelector('#target'); const result = await snapdom(el); const img = await result.toPng(); document.body.appendChild(img); await result.download({ format: 'jpg', filename: 'my-capture' }); ``` ##### [One-step shortcuts](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-one-step-shortcuts) Copy code ``` const el = document.querySelector('#target'); const png = await snapdom.toPng(el); document.body.appendChild(png); const blob = await snapdom.toBlob(el); ``` #### [API](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-api) ##### [`snapdom(el, options?)`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-snapdomel-options) Returns an object with reusable export methods: Copy code ``` { url: string; toRaw(): string; toImg(): Promise; toCanvas(): Promise; toBlob(options?): Promise; toPng(options?): Promise; toJpg(options?): Promise; toWebp(options?): Promise; download(options?): Promise; } ``` ##### [Shortcut methods](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-shortcut-methods) | Method | Description | | --- | --- | | `snapdom.toImg(el, options?)` | Returns an `HTMLImageElement` | | `snapdom.toCanvas(el, options?)` | Returns a `Canvas` | | `snapdom.toBlob(el, options?)` | Returns an SVG or raster `Blob` | | `snapdom.toPng(el, options?)` | Returns a PNG image | | `snapdom.toJpg(el, options?)` | Returns a JPG image | | `snapdom.toWebp(el, options?)` | Returns a WebP image | | `snapdom.download(el, options?)` | Triggers a download | #### [Options](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-options) > ✅ **Note:** Style compression is now always on internally. The `compress` option has been removed. All capture methods accept an `options` object: | Option | Type | Default | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `fast` | boolean | `true` | Skips small idle delays for faster results | | `embedFonts` | boolean | `false` | Inlines non-icon fonts (icon fonts always on) | | `localFonts` | array | `[]` | Local fonts `{ family, src, weight?, style? }` | | `iconFonts` | string\|RegExp\|Array | `[]` | Extra icon font matchers | | `excludeFonts` | object | `{}` | Exclude font families/domains/subsets during embedding | | `scale` | number | `1` | Output scale multiplier | | `dpr` | number | `devicePixelRatio` | Device pixel ratio | | `width` | number | - | Output width | | `height` | number | - | Output height | | `backgroundColor` | string | `"#fff"` | Fallback color for JPG/WebP | | `quality` | number | `1` | Quality for JPG/WebP (0 to 1) | | `useProxy` | string | `''` | Proxy base for CORS fallbacks | | `type` | string | `svg` | Default Blob type (`svg`\|`png`\|`jpg`\|`webp`) | | `exclude` | string\[\] | - | CSS selectors to exclude | | `filter` | function | - | Custom predicate `(el) => boolean` | | `cache` | string | `"soft"` | Control internal caches: `disabled`, `soft`, `auto`, `full` | | `defaultImageUrl` | string \| function | - | Fallback image when an `` fails. If a function is provided, it receives `{ width?, height?, src?, element }` and must return a URL (string or Promise). Useful for placeholder services (e.g. `https://placehold.co/{width}x{height}`) | ##### [Fallback image on `` load failure](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-fallback-image-on-img-load-failure) Provide a default image for failed `` loads. You can pass a fixed URL or a callback that receives measured dimensions and returns a URL (handy to generate dynamic placeholders). Copy code ``` // 1) Fixed URL fallback await snapdom.toImg(element, { defaultImageUrl: '/images/fallback.png' }); // 2) Dynamic placeholder via callback await snapdom.toImg(element, { defaultImageUrl: ({ width = 300, height = 150 }) => `https://placehold.co/${Math.round(width)}x${Math.round(height)}` }); // 3) With proxy (if your fallback host has no CORS) await snapdom.toImg(element, { defaultImageUrl: ({ width = 300, height = 150 }) => `https://dummyimage.com/${Math.round(width)}x${Math.round(height)}/cccccc/666.png&text=img`, useProxy: 'https://corsproxy.io/?url=' }); ``` Notes: - If the fallback image also fails to load, snapDOM replaces the `` with a placeholder block preserving width/height. - Width/height used by the callback are gathered from the original element (dataset, style/attrs, etc.) when available. ##### [Dimensions (`scale`, `width`, `height`)](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-dimensions-scale-width-height) - If `scale` is provided, it **takes precedence** over `width`/`height`. - If only `width` is provided, height scales proportionally (and vice versa). - Providing both `width` and `height` forces an exact size (may distort). ##### [Cross-Origin Images & Fonts (`useProxy`)](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-cross-origin-images-fonts-useproxy) By default snapDOM tries `crossOrigin="anonymous"` (or `use-credentials` for same-origin). If an asset is CORS-blocked, you can set `useProxy` to a prefix URL that forwards the actual `src`: Copy code ``` await snapdom.toPng(el, { useProxy: 'your-proxy' // Example 'https://api.allorigins.win/raw?url=' }); ``` **Tips** - Keep the proxy **fast** and **cache-friendly** (adds big wins on repeated captures). - The proxy is only used as a **fallback**; same-origin and CORS-enabled assets skip it. ##### [Fonts](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-fonts) ###### [`embedFonts`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-embedfonts) When `true`, snapDOM embeds **non-icon**`@font-face` rules detected as used within the captured subtree. Icon fonts (Font Awesome, Material Icons, etc.) are embedded **always**. ###### [`localFonts`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-localfonts) If you serve fonts yourself or have data URLs, you can declare them here to avoid extra CSS discovery: Copy code ``` await snapdom.toPng(el, { embedFonts: true, localFonts: [\ { family: 'Inter', src: '/fonts/Inter-Variable.woff2', weight: 400, style: 'normal' },\ { family: 'Inter', src: '/fonts/Inter-Italic.woff2', style: 'italic' }\ ] }); ``` ###### [`iconFonts`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-iconfonts) Add custom icon families (names or regex matchers). Useful for private icon sets: Copy code ``` await snapdom.toPng(el, { iconFonts: ['MyIcons', /^(Remix|Feather) Icons?$/i] }); ``` ###### [`excludeFonts`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-excludefonts) Skip specific non-icon fonts to speed up capture or avoid unnecessary downloads. Copy code ``` await snapdom.toPng(el, { embedFonts: true, excludeFonts: { families: ['Noto Serif', 'SomeHeavyFont'], // skip by family name domains: ['fonts.gstatic.com', 'cdn.example'], // skip by source host subsets: ['cyrillic-ext'] // skip by unicode-range subset tag } }); ``` _Notes_ - `excludeFonts` only applies to **non-icon** fonts. Icon fonts are always embedded. - Matching is case-insensitive for `families`. Hosts are matched by substring against the resolved URL. ##### [Filtering nodes: `exclude` vs `filter`](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-filtering-nodes-exclude-vs-filter) - `exclude`: remove by **selector**. - `filter`: advanced predicate per element (return `false` to drop). **Example: filter out elements with `display:none`:** Copy code ``` /** * Example filter: skip elements with display:none * @param {Element} el * @returns {boolean} true = keep, false = exclude */ function filterHidden(el) { const cs = window.getComputedStyle(el); if (cs.display === 'none') return false; return true; } await snapdom.toPng(document.body, { filter: filterHidden }); ``` **Example with `exclude`:** remove banners or tooltips by selector Copy code ``` await snapdom.toPng(el, { exclude: ['.cookie-banner', '.tooltip', '[data-test="debug"]'] }); ``` ##### [`preCache()` – Optional helper](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-precache-optional-helper) Preloads external resources to avoid first-capture stalls (helpful for big/complex trees). Copy code ``` import { preCache } from '@zumer/snapdom'; await preCache({ root: document.body, embedFonts: true, localFonts: [{ family: 'Inter', src: '/fonts/Inter.woff2', weight: 400 }], useProxy: 'your-proxy' }); ``` ##### [Cache control](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-cache-control) SnapDOM maintains internal caches for images, backgrounds, resources, styles, and fonts. You can control how they are cleared between captures using the `cache` option: | Mode | Description | | --- | --- | | `"disabled"` | No cache | | `"soft"` | Clears session caches (`styleMap`, `nodeMap`, `styleCache`) _(default)_ | | `"auto"` | Minimal cleanup: only clears transient maps | | `"full"` | Keeps all caches (nothing is cleared, maximum performance) | **Examples:** Copy code ``` // Use minimal but fast cache await snapdom.toPng(el, { cache: 'auto' }); // Keep everything in memory between captures await snapdom.toPng(el, { cache: 'full' }); // Force a full cleanup on every capture await snapdom.toPng(el, { cache: 'disabled' }); ``` #### [Limitations](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-limitations) - External images should be CORS-accessible (use `useProxy` option for handling CORS denied) - When WebP format is used on Safari, it will fallback to PNG rendering. - `@font-face` CSS rule is well supported, but if need to use JS `FontFace()`, see this workaround [`#43`](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/issues/43) #### [⚡ Performance Benchmarks](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-performance-benchmarks) Snapdom has received **significant performance improvements** since version `v1.8.0`. The following benchmarks compare: - **Snapdom (current)** - **Snapdom v1.8.0** - `html2canvas` - `html-to-image` ##### [Simple elements](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-simple-elements) | Scenario | Snapdom (current) | Snapdom v1.8.0 | html2canvas | html-to-image | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Small (200×100) | **0.4 ms** | 1.2 ms | 70.3 ms | 3.6 ms | | Modal (400×300) | **0.4 ms** | 1.1 ms | 68.8 ms | 3.6 ms | | Page View (1200×800) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 100.5 ms | 3.4 ms | | Large Scroll (2000×1500) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 153.1 ms | 3.4 ms | | Very Large (4000×2000) | **0.4 ms** | 1.0 ms | 278.9 ms | 4.3 ms | ##### [Complex elements](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-complex-elements) | Scenario | Snapdom (current) | Snapdom v1.8.0 | html2canvas | html-to-image | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Small (200×100) | **1.1 ms** | 3.2 ms | 76.0 ms | 15.3 ms | | Modal (400×300) | **4.5 ms** | 14.0 ms | 133.2 ms | 55.4 ms | | Page View (1200×800) | **32.9 ms** | 113.6 ms | 303.4 ms | 369.1 ms | | Large Scroll (2000×1500) | **133.9 ms** | 387.4 ms | 594.4 ms | 1,163.0 ms | | Very Large (4000×2000) | **364.0 ms** | 1,200.4 ms | 1,380.8 ms | 3,023.9 ms | ##### [Summary](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-summary) - Snapdom (current) is **2×–6× faster** than `v1.8.0` - Up to **150× faster** than `html2canvas` - Up to **8× faster** than `html-to-image` in large scenarios Benchmarks run in Chromium using Vitest. Hardware: MacBook Air 2018. ⚠️ Performance may vary depending on device. ##### [Run the benchmarks](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-run-the-benchmarks) Copy code ``` git clone https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom.git cd snapdom npm install npm run test:benchmark ``` #### [Roadmap](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-roadmap) Planned improvements for future versions of SnapDOM: - **Implement plugin system** SnapDOM will support external plugins to extend or override internal behavior (e.g. custom node transformers, exporters, or filters). - **Refactor to modular architecture** Internal logic will be split into smaller, focused modules to improve maintainability and code reuse. - **Decouple internal logic from global options** Functions will be redesigned to avoid relying directly on `options`. A centralized capture context will improve clarity, autonomy, and testability. See [`next` branch](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/tree/main) - **Expose cache control** Users will be able to manually clear image and font caches or configure their own caching strategies. - **Auto font preloading** Required fonts will be automatically detected and preloaded before capture, reducing the need for manual `preCache()` calls. - **Document plugin development** A full guide will be provided for creating and registering custom SnapDOM plugins. - **Make export utilities tree-shakeable** Export functions like `toPng`, `toJpg`, `toBlob`, etc. will be restructured into independent modules to support tree shaking and minimal builds. Have ideas or feature requests? Feel free to share suggestions or feedback in [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/discussions). #### [Development](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-development) To contribute or build snapDOM locally: Copy code ``` # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom.git cd snapdom # Switch to dev branch git checkout dev # Install dependencies npm install # Compile the library (ESM, CJS, and minified versions) npm run compile # Install playwright browsers (necessary for running tests) npx playwright install # Run tests npm test # Run Benchmarks npm run test:benchmark ``` The main entry point is in `src/`, and output bundles are generated in the `dist/` folder. For detailed contribution guidelines, please see [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/zumerlab/snapdom/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md). #### [Contributors If you'd like to support this project too, you can [become a sponsor](https://github.com/sponsors/tinchox5). #### [Star History](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-star-history) [![Star History Chart](https://npmx.dev/api/registry/image-proxy?url=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.star-history.com%2Fsvg%3Frepos%3Dzumerlab%2Fsnapdom%26type%3DDate&sig=2b13af40746f8fbabe280bafe2f897a91ca5006cc9650188af6baac915c7af0f)](https://www.star-history.com/#zumerlab/snapdom&Date) #### [License](https://npmx.dev/package/@zumer/snapdom/v/1.9.10-dev.2\#user-content-license) MIT © Zumerlab ## keyboard shortcuts Use the command palette to jump between pages, package views, settings, and external links without leaving the keyboard. On macOS, press ⌘K. On Windows and Linux, press Ctrl+K. Global - `Ctrl+K` Open command palette - `/` Focus search - `?` Highlight keyboard hints - `,` Open settings - `c` Open compare Search - `↑`/ `↓` Navigate results - `Enter` Go to result Package - `m` Open main information - `d` Open docs - `.` Open code view - `f` Open version differences - `t` Open timeline - `c` Open compare (prefilled with current package) You can disable keyboard shortcuts in [settings](https://npmx.dev/settings). ## Local Connector Run the connector on your machine to enable admin features. $npx npmx-connector npmpnpmyarnbundenovltvp Then paste the token below to connect: Token Advanced options Port Automatically open auth page WARNING This allows npmx to access your npm CLI. Only connect to sites you trust. Connect ## command palette navigate across npmx and switch settings quickly ## Agent Skills ### Install skills-npm skills CLI Compatible with `skills-npm` [Learn more](https://npmx.dev/package/skills-npm) ### Available Skills Click to expand ## Atmosphere Connect with your Atmosphere account Handle What is an Atmosphere account? npmx.dev uses the [AT Protocol](https://atproto.com/) to power many of its social features, allowing users to own their data and use one account for all compatible applications. Once you create an account, you can use other apps like [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/) and [Tangled](https://tangled.org/) with the same account. ConnectCreate a new account * * * Connect with Bluesky
